rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE JESUS O. PULE AND AMALIA PULE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition (1) For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration; 2) For An Accounting 3) For An Order To Distribute Trust Assets; 4) For Damages Due To
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The notice of hearing (with far more than just the caption and a brief description and not in 12 point type, as required by CRC 2.104) was mailed on 10/31/25 for this hearing. Trusts require 30 days' notice. (2) How is Nohemi Ochoa the successor in interest to Fortu[a]nato Ochoa? Was she appointed as a personal representative for his estate or is she the successor trustee of his trust? By 1/7/26, please provide the assigned examiner a courtesy copy of (1) the supplement to the petition, with proof of how Nohemi Ochoa is the successor in interest to Fortu[a]nato Ochoa and (2) the ONE proof of service of Notice of Hearing, showing that notice was mailed at least 30 days before 1/21/26. (The ONLY correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is used, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served, along with the attached instructions for how to appear remotely, must be attached to that proof of service.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHRYN E BASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was just filed on 11/12/25 (e-filed on 11/13/25), which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) An amended petition requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including publication. [CRC 7.53.] (3) Item 2c of the amended petition has both full and limited authority checked. (4) Item 3a of the amended petition has both boxes (1) and (2) checked. (5) The waiver of bond is a mandatory Judicial Council form. (6) Item 3g(2)(a) of the amended petition is not actually correct; the proposed personal representative is a nominee of a person entitled to Letters. (7) Item 3h of the amended petition has both boxes (1) and (2) checked. (8) Item 5 of the amended petition is nonsensical, as is item 7. (9) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/7/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
